mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains below 4.0% and declines month-over-month, signaling monetary accommodation. Lower policy rates reduce equity discount rates and increase net present value of corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary accommodation or early tightening cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in < 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 4.0% and declining.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a 3-month window, signaling labor market deterioration. Equity investors interpret rising joblessness as a leading indicator of demand weakness and corporate profit compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in < 50% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) remain flat or decline, indicating synchronized weakness in consumer and residential demand. This dual contraction signals reduced corporate revenue growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1% MoM AND HOUST declines > 2% MoM
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in < 52% of instances when both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 102.5 index level and maintains month-over-month growth > 0.3%, signaling robust supply-side capacity utilization. Strong production growth supports corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with strong productivity
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in < 54% of instances when INDPRO > 102.5 with MoM growth > 0.3%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to < 100K new jobs per month while unemployment rate remains stable, indicating labor market slack emerging despite headline employment stability. Equity investors interpret slowing job creation as a leading indicator of demand weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth < 100K AND UNRATE stable (±0.1pp)
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market deceleration
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in < 51% of instances when PAYEMS growth < 100K and UNRATE stable, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 334 index level while federal funds rate remains below 4.0%, creating negative real rates. Equity investors interpret this as monetary policy lag and future tightening risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as rate hike expectations rise.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in < 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL > 334 and FEDFUNDS < 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains above 24000 billion chained 2017 dollars while housing starts (HOUST) decline > 5% quarter-over-quarter, indicating demand composition shift away from residential investment. This reallocation signals late-cycle dynamics and equity valuation pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with residential demand weakness
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in < 52% of instances when GDPC1 > 24000 and HOUST declines > 5% QoQ, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while payroll employment (PAYEMS) remains flat or grows < 50K, indicating synchronized supply-side and labor market weakness. This dual contraction signals recession risk and corporate profit compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.2% MoM AND PAYEMS growth < 50K
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in < 53% of instances when INDPRO declines > 0.2% MoM and PAYEMS growth < 50K, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
